units.title: Orientation
units.objective: Set expectations for restored Attic and the on-ramp.
units.taught: What restored Attic is and why this app teaches it; the 'ancient texts were ALL CAPS with no spaces' reveal; the promise that the reader + tap-to-parse will carry you.

units.title: Familiar letters
units.objective: Read/sound 7 letters; read first real syllables.
units.taught: Letterform + name + restored sound + lowercase handwriting.
units.srs_feed: Each letter becomes a 3-card family (letter→sound, sound→letter, name).
units.advance: ≥90% recognition across a session; all 7 cards reach first successful review.

units.title: Distinctive Greek letters
units.objective: Add 10 letters incl. aspirates φ/θ and double ψ.
units.taught: Forms, restored sounds, aspirate contrast (audio minimal pairs), the two sigmas, multi-stroke ψ/θ handwriting.
units.srs_feed: New letter cards interleave with Unit 1.

units.title: False friends
units.taught: Each letter paired against its Latin twin (Η≠h/n, Ν≠v, Ρ≠p, Χ≠x, Υ≠u, Ζ); η=[ɛː], υ=[y], ζ=[zd].
units.srs_feed: Cards flagged higher-difficulty; FSRS schedules them more often.
units.advance: ≥90% on a mixed full-alphabet recognition quiz.

units.title: Vowel quantity & diphthongs
units.objective: Hear/encode long vs short and the 8 diphthongs.
units.taught: ε/η and ο/ω as length+quality pairs; hidden quantity of α/ι/υ (macron/breve); diphthong values; genuine vs spurious ει/ου = pure long vowels; iota subscript preview.
units.srs_feed: Diphthongs as cards; quantity contrasts as listening cards.

units.title: Breathings & the sound system in words
units.objective: Read whole short real words aloud.
units.taught: Rough vs smooth breathing; initial ρ/υ rules; breathing on diphthongs/capitals; γ-nasal; ρ trill.
units.srs_feed: Begin DCC core vocab as lemma cards (form + sound + gloss), highest-frequency ~50 first.
units.advance: Read 10 core words with correct breathings/sounds.

units.title: Accents (recognition)
units.objective: Recognize the three marks; know they were musical — no production pressure.
units.taught: Acute/grave/circumflex = pitch; circumflex only on long vowel/diphthong; acute→grave in context; one honest slide on reconstruction uncertainty.
units.advance: Name the three accents; judge 8/10 'same word?' accent items.

units.title: How Greek words work
units.objective: Internalize inflection + lemma so tap-to-parse makes sense.
units.taught: Inflection via the λόγος paradigm; the five cases conceptually; lemma vs form; word-order freedom.
units.srs_feed: Core vocab continues as lemmas; add a few form→lemma cards.
units.advance: Identify lemma vs inflected form for 8/10 tapped words; explain why endings matter.

units.title: Variation, punctuation & handoff to Euthyphro
units.objective: Not be confused by the three kinds of variation or Greek punctuation; begin reading.
units.taught: The three variation types (inflection / accent-in-context / orthographic-dialectal); punctuation (; = question mark, · raised dot), scriptio continua, iota subscript — each with a Euthyphro example (ἐγᾦμαι, τί φῄς;, ὦ Σώκρατες).
units.srs_feed: Euthyphro opening vocabulary seeded into FSRS.
units.advance: Read Euthyphro 2a with parse support and self-rate ≥'mostly understood'.
